text:
Once there was a cat who knew everything. She knew all the birds' songs, all the wind sounds, all the secrets of the forest. Her name was Seraphina.

One rainy evening, as Seraphina p prowled through a wood singing her secret lore, she stumbled onto a pond. Curious kittens gathered round her. "Oh," she said softly, "can't you see I know the moon songs? Let me teach you."

The kittens loved her tales. Every kitten had its own favourite whisper from Seraphina.

Late nights they'd sing along.

The first was a chipmunk's happy bark.

The fifth was an owl that didn't mind Seraphina's tales told when it was sleepily scanning the night.

That's when the wise old owl named Olaf showed up.

"How many songs," Olaf inquired.

"One," Seraphina sighed, "the only one I'd keep secret."

Olaf listened intently for a while before he said
